title: Merge Gate never evaluates on fresh PRs: missing 'opened' trigger + bot-token label-sync doesn't cascade

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Fresh continuation lane opened directly from current main. Carries forward root cause #1 (bot-token cascade fix, previously implemented and PM-reviewed on PR #1247) re-applied cleanly, and adds root cause #2 (merge-gate.yml opened trigger) as new work in this same commit -- the prior PR deferred root cause #2 only because merge-gate.yml was held in a concurrent lane's file-scope lock, and is now stuck at its T1 bounce cap and CONFLICTING against current main, which is why this is a new PR rather than a rebase.
sha_binding.merge_sha_note: PR #1264 merged to main as 09f08701848f21cb7949b912134868bb3a5d88b5 on 2026-07-20. This repair binds the evidence bundle to that authoritative merge SHA so P3/C4 (proof SHA reference) pass; it does not change the reviewed implementation.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Not independent Griff review, t1-approved, or a pm-verdict/v1 owner artifact.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD

runtime_proof.note: This lane changes only CI workflow trigger/authentication configuration; no production code path or migration touched. Live smoke run (against real Supabase, apps/api/src/database-smoke.test.ts) executed regardless per T1 policy.

queries.description: INSERT via processSubmission — one row per synthetic pick, in the 6 of 7 tests that create a pick (all except the read-only UTV2-883 participants test)
INSERT via processSubmission (same 6 tests); UPDATE lifecycle_state via transitionPickLifecycle (queued, posted) and via the settlement RPC path

INSERT on each successful transitionPickLifecycle call and on each successful settlement transition; SELECT COUNT(*) used by the two UTV2-920 rejection tests to assert zero rows were written for the target to_state after a rejected atomic operation

INSERT via one successful enqueueDistributionAtomic call (UTV2-920 invalid-delivery test setup); UPDATE status to 'processing' via claimNextAtomic; a separate enqueue attempt (UTV2-920 invalid-enqueue test) is rejected and asserted via findByIdempotencyKey returning null
SELECT COUNT(*) used to assert zero rows were written after a rejected confirmDeliveryAtomic call — no receipt row is created anywhere in this test run

SELECT COUNT(*) used by the UTV2-920 rejection tests to assert zero audit rows were written for the rejected action (distribution.sent, settlement); real audit inserts happen inside the atomic RPCs on the successful paths (test 1, test 6, test 7)

INSERT via recordPickSettlement/settlePickAtomic on successful calls; one settlePickAtomic call (UTV2-920 invalid settlement) is rejected and asserted via SELECT COUNT(*) returning zero; SELECT used to assert exactly one base record (corrects_id null) and that a correction chain is additive, not mutating
SELECT via repositories.participants.listByType('player') — read-only invariant check (UTV2-883), no writes

supersedes.reason: Prior branch hit the T1 3-CHANGES_REQUIRED bounce cap and is CONFLICTING against current main after an unrelated concurrent lane (which held merge-gate.yml's file-scope lock) merged. Per instruction, this lane does not resolve those conflicts -- it reimplements both fixes cleanly from current main in a new branch/PR, carrying forward the prior PR's accepted part-1 content and completing the previously deferred part 2 (the opened trigger) on top.
